english: Brain
Your brain is about 73% water — made of hydrogen and oxygen!
73% water, electrical signals
Water that cushions and feeds brain cells
Building block of every neuron and brain cell
Part of water and all the fats that insulate your nerves
Found in neurotransmitters — the chemical messengers
Powers ATP, the energy molecule your brain burns through
Creates electrical signals so neurons can talk to each other
Works with potassium to fire nerve signals
Carries oxygen to the brain through your blood
Thyroid
This tiny butterfly-shaped gland in your neck controls your metabolism using iodine!
Needs iodine to work
Essential — the thyroid is the only organ that absorbs iodine to make hormones
Protects the thyroid from damage and helps convert hormones
Water keeps the gland working properly
Makes up the hormone molecules themselves
Part of the amino acid tyrosine used to build thyroid hormones
Helps the body use thyroid hormones correctly
Heart
Your heart beats about 100,000 times a day — powered by electrical signals from ions!
100,000 beats per day
Controls your heartbeat rhythm — too little and your heart skips!
Makes heart muscles squeeze with each beat
Creates the electrical wave that triggers each heartbeat
Fuel for the hard-working heart muscle
Builds the muscle fibers of the heart wall
Keeps the heartbeat steady and prevents cramps
In the blood flowing through — carries oxygen everywhere
Lungs
Your lungs have a surface area the size of a tennis court — all to swap O₂ and CO₂!
Tennis-court surface area
The whole reason lungs exist — pulling oxygen from the air!
You breathe OUT carbon as CO₂ with every exhale
In the water vapor you breathe out and the mucus lining
78% of what you inhale is nitrogen (but your body ignores it)
Part of surfactant — the soap-like coating that keeps lungs inflated
In the proteins that give lung tissue its stretchy structure
Stomach & Organs
Your stomach acid is strong enough to dissolve metal — thanks to hydrochloric acid!
Acid that dissolves metal
Makes hydrochloric acid (HCl) to break down food
The other half of stomach acid — hydrogen ions!
In water, bile, and digestive enzymes
Builds the enzymes that chop up your food
Found in every digestive enzyme (they're all proteins)
Needed to make digestive enzymes work properly
In the bicarbonate that protects your stomach lining from its own acid!
Muscles
You have over 600 muscles — they're mostly water, protein, and a dash of minerals!
600+ muscles, mostly water
Muscles are about 75% water
Builds the protein fibers (actin and myosin) that contract
Part of water and every protein strand
Muscles are mostly protein — and nitrogen is key to every amino acid
Triggers muscle contraction — every flex starts with calcium!
Prevents cramps and helps muscles relax after contracting
Helps muscles relax — low magnesium = cramps!
In the amino acids that give muscles their structure
Bones & Teeth
Your bones are alive! They constantly rebuild themselves using calcium and phosphorus.
Alive & always rebuilding
The star of the show — makes bones hard and strong!
Combines with calcium to form hydroxyapatite — the crystal in bones
Part of the mineral crystal structure and water in bone marrow
In the collagen fibers that give bones flexibility
Makes up about 1% of bone — helps with bone density
Hardens tooth enamel — that's why it's in toothpaste!
Your bones contain tiny amounts — it behaves like calcium!
Blood
You have about 5 liters of blood — and its red color comes from iron!
5 liters, iron = red color
The iron in hemoglobin grabs oxygen and carries it everywhere — AND makes blood red!
Blood is mostly water — and it carries dissolved oxygen to every cell
Part of water and helps maintain blood pH
Builds hemoglobin, white blood cells, and clotting proteins
The reason blood tastes salty! Regulates blood pressure
Partners with sodium to keep blood volume balanced
Keeps blood pressure in check alongside sodium
Helps the body absorb iron — copper deficiency = anemia
Supports white blood cells that fight infections
Skin & Hair
Your skin is your largest organ — about 2 square meters of it! Hair and nails are made of keratin protein.
Largest organ, 2m²
Builds keratin — the tough protein in skin, hair, and nails
In the water that keeps skin hydrated and flexible
Part of water and every protein in your skin
In the amino acids that build keratin and collagen
Makes disulfide bonds — the reason curly hair keeps its shape!
Helps skin heal and fights acne — zinc cream is a real thing!
Protects skin from sun damage
